desc: Suncheon is a green city known for its bay wetlands, gardens, and easy rail access to Korea's southern coast and tea-country day trips.
hls: Suncheon Bay Wetland Reserve and reed-field sunsets
Well-planned parks and urban green corridors
Gateway to Boseong tea fields and Yeosu coast
Good-value alternative to Seoul/Busan city breaks

todo.name: Suncheon Bay wetlands boardwalk
todo.desc: Walk reed trails and birdwatching hides near high tide.

todo.name: Suncheonman National Garden
todo.desc: Explore themed gardens and seasonal floral displays.

todo.name: Naganeupseong Folk Village
todo.desc: Visit preserved Joseon-era fortress village architecture.

todo.name: Yeosu day trip
todo.desc: Use rail for sea cable car and harbor walks.

todo.name: Boseong green tea fields
todo.desc: Pair tea plantation views with tasting stops.

wifi.note: Excellent Korea-wide connectivity including public Wi-Fi in transit nodes.

prac.fltNote: No direct UK flights to Suncheon itself; travelers usually enter via Seoul (often nonstop from London) then continue by KTX/ITX rail or domestic connection, with spring blossom and autumn foliage peaks.
prac.currency: South Korea local currency (check live rates before booking).
prac.lang: Local language plus tourism-level English in main visitor areas.
prac.tz: Check local timezone and DST rules for exact travel dates.
prac.visa: Confirm current entry/transit rules for your passport before booking.
alerts: Wetland walkways can close temporarily in typhoon-adjacent weather.
Intercity rail is frequent, but last-return services from coast cities can be earlier than expected on weekdays.
Summer humidity is intense—plan outdoor wetland walks for mornings/evenings.

sweet: Apr-May and Oct for mild weather, flowers/reeds, and comfortable walking conditions.
